subject: LU-9049 obdclass: change object lookup to no wait mode

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

I'd rather suggest to try to remove that "block-until-released". the original reason was that we didn't handle destroyed objects properly (methods like dt_attr_get(), etc). but we do now. so logically it should be OK to return just destroyed objects.

Patch Set 5:

Di, do you think the latest fix is correct?

The one you added in out_handler.c? it looks good to me?  Sorry I did not check other part yet.

Patch Set 5:

I'd like to understand the root cause for the deadlock, etc..

Patch Set 5:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 5:

Deadlock? Sorry I might miss the context here.  Only marking "creating" object to be BANSEE in OUT is because it is incomplete. see my comments above.

I am not sure about deadlock.  Lai, what deadlock did you met?

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

The original cause of deadlock in LU-9049 is that lookup object will wait if object is marked dying, because there is no strict ordering for ldlm lock and object lookup, one process can hold lock then lookup object, another one may take object refcount, mark it dying, then request ldlm lock, so system may deadlock.

Because current code can handle dying object already, there is no need to wait for such object. But as Di said, object created in OUT is not complete in all layers, and shouldn't be used in MDT context, so we'll mark such object dying after creation, and others wait for its last put.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

I think Di means lod_object is not initialized in OUT creation, e.g. lod stripe info and sub objects.

Patch Set 5:

i'm  not sure how this is possible - how LOD knows we initialize from OUT, rather than from MDT ?

Patch Set 5:

I remember when an existing object is being initialized (e.g. to process unlink), then striping isn't initialized right away (because that can result in an additional I/O), but it's initialized at ->declare_destroy().
IOW, I'd expect partly-initialized objects to work.. or would try to fix that to work this way, if possible.

Patch Set 5:

OUT is built directly above OSD. there is no LOD between them.

Patch Set 5:

Lai said we use site's top layer (MDT) when a new object is being created. did I get it wrong?

Patch Set 5:

I think Lai means creating the objec in memory, i.e. when it use dt_locate(), it will create a normal object with full stack. 
But if you do out_create, see out_tx_create_exec(), it will call into OSD directly.

Patch Set 5:

then why can't we use dt_locate() in create path as well?

Patch Set 5:

Alex, out create uses dt_locate() to allocate obj, and it's initialized in all layers, but it calls dt_create() to create this object which bypasses LOD layer so stripe is not set. Do you mean out_tx_create_exec() should calls md_create()? But in my understanding OUT should be right above OSD?

Patch Set 5:

"no, I mean dt_locate() (starting from from the top - MDT), then osd_create(). did you try this?"

but lod_create/lod_declare_create() will not be called anyway, then the object is incomplete. Or I miss sth here?

Patch Set 6:

please be more specific. yes, lod_create() won't be called, but in this specific case we don't create striping with local LOD, so what exactly we'll be missing with lod_create() absent?

Patch Set 6:

Hmm, see lod_object_start(), do_body_ops will not be filled for new object, because loh_attr is 0 for new object( I assume unless this changed?). So it seems these do_body_ops will be reset in lod_declare_object_create(). Not sure how to fit this in out_create process.

Though I did not check other cases. not sure if this is the only one.

Patch Set 6:

if this is the only reason, then I'd suggest to check-and-set in other methods so we can get them instantiated when needed.

Patch Set 6:

ok, I'll tweak the code like this and test.

Patch Set 6:

I understand "killing incomplete object" is a bit tricky, but imho "check and set" in lod would be more tricky here.

Patch Set 6:

killing incomplete object doesn't work AFAIU because it can deadlock.

Patch Set 6:

Hmm, I am confused, I thought lai's change already fix the deadlock? no?

Patch Set 6:

I think Lai can clarify. my opinion is that all these "block-until" can still lead to deadlocks in corner cases which we'd like to avoid as a whole.

Patch Set 6:

Yes, in theory there can be case which leads to deadlock, I'm tweaking the code to see whether incomplete obj can be handled correctly by subsequent operations other than removing it from cache.

parents.subject: LU-9485 llite: check the return value of cl_file_inode_init()

commit.subject: LU-9049 obdclass: change object lookup to no wait mode
commit.message:
LU-9049 obdclass: change object lookup to no wait mode

Currently we set LU_OBJECT_HEARD_BANSHEE on object when we want
to remove object from cache, but this may lead to deadlock, because
when other process lookup such object, it needs to wait for this
object until release (done at last refcount put), while that process
maybe already hold an LDLM lock.

Now that current code can handle dying object correctly, we can just
return such object in lookup, thus the above deadlock can be avoided.

There is another case we need to make some changes:
objects created in OUT doesn't set dt_body_ops for LOD layer because
originally it's set by lod_create(), change to set dt_body_ops in
lod_object_init() so objects created in OUT is no different from
those created in MDT. To achieve this, functions in lod_body_ops
should check file type inside to avoid misuse.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
